model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Florida and Houston are separated by a negligible 0.28-point adjusted efficiency margin (KenPom #4 vs. #5, Torvik #4 vs. #5), making this the most evenly matched Elite Eight game in the bracket on raw metrics alone. The decisive tiebreakers all favor Houston: the Cougars are playing at Toyota Center — their literal home arena — providing a structural crowd and familiarity advantage worth several points in what is nominally a neutral-site setting, and they carry powerful revenge motivation after losing to Florida in the 2025 national championship game. Houston's defense ranks 4th nationally (AdjDE 91.0), marginally superior to Florida's 5th-ranked unit, and Kelvin Sampson's grind-it-out defensive system is specifically built to neutralize high-powered offenses like Florida's top-9 attack. The combination of home-court advantage, revenge narrative, elite defensive identity, and clutch tournament execution — including a last-second 62-60 win over Purdue in the Sweet 16 — make the Cougars the narrow right side in what is effectively a coin-flip game.

reasoningStep.title: South Regional Final: Florida vs. Houston — Holistic Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Florida and Houston are statistically near-identical by every major efficiency metric, making contextual factors — home court, revenge motivation, defensive identity, and recent form — the decisive tiebreakers. Houston's home-arena advantage at Toyota Center and their revenge motivation from the 2025 national championship loss to Florida tip this razor-thin matchup to the Cougars despite Florida's marginal offensive edge and reigning champion pedigree.
evidence: Florida: KenPom #4 (AdjEM +33.83), Torvik #4 (AdjOE 126.1, AdjDE 92.2). Houston: KenPom #5 (AdjEM +33.55), Torvik #5 (AdjOE 125.1, AdjDE 91.9). The efficiency gap is ~0.28 points — statistically negligible and within any model's margin of error, making this a true coin-flip on raw numbers.

Houston's defense ranks 4th nationally (AdjDE 91.0 by KenPom) vs. Florida's 5th (AdjDE 91.8). Houston's defensive identity under Kelvin Sampson — slowing possessions, forcing turnovers, limiting transition — is the single most dangerous weapon against Florida's top-9 offense.

The South Regional Sweet 16 and Elite Eight are hosted at Toyota Center in Houston — the Cougars' literal home arena. This structural crowd and familiarity advantage is worth an estimated 3-4 points in what is nominally a neutral-site game, a decisive edge in a matchup this close.

Houston lost to Florida in the 2025 national championship game. Lineups.com explicitly cited this revenge narrative in picking Houston to beat Florida in the Elite Eight, and the emotional stakes and familiarity with Florida's system give the Cougars a meaningful preparation and motivation edge.

Houston beat Purdue (KenPom #8) 62-60 on a perfectly executed last-second inbounds play in the Sweet 16, demonstrating elite clutch execution and tournament resilience. Florida advanced on Alex Condon's return from injury, introducing mild health uncertainty going forward.

Pre-tournament odds had Houston at +240 to reach the Final Four from the South, and expert consensus (Lineups.com South Region breakdown) explicitly picked Houston to send Florida home in the Elite Eight, citing home-court and revenge factors as the decisive tiebreakers.
